JavaScript Core (82) — Async/Await: Writing Reusable Error Catching

Wrapping every async/await call in its own try...catch gets old fast. In this post we walk through pulling the catch logic out into a single reusable function — and then fixing the version that breaks the moment you need to pass arguments in, using a bit of closure magic.
Does let Get Hoisted? Meet the Temporal Dead Zone (JS Core #61)

Everyone knows var gets hoisted — but what about let? This post digs into whether let is hoisted too, why calling it early throws a ReferenceError instead of giving you undefined, and what the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) actually is. We'll also use a typeof trick to prove let really does get hoisted, just in a different way.
JavaScript Core Concepts: The Full Series Index (and Why I Wrote It)

This is the table of contents and intro for my JavaScript Core Concepts series, which I wrote while going through Hexschool's online video course. I'd already gone through the weird parts of JavaScript before, but every instructor explains things their own way — so I figured I'd take notes and learn it again from scratch. This page links to every article in the series.
